title: sida elongata var. balica
intro: Sida elongata var. balica is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family, commonly known as the hibiscus or mallow family. It is native to the Philippines and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain and swelling, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Sida elongata var. balica can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments.
benefits: Sida elongata var. balica has many benefits, such as its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes due to its high antioxidant and flavonoid content. Additionally, its antifungal properties can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing, while its anti-cancer properties may inhibit the growth of cancer cells. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which can improve cognitive function and reduce the risk of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Overall, the benefits of Sida elongata var. balica can significantly improve quality of life by reducing the risk of chronic diseases and promoting overall health and well-being.
constituents: Sida elongata var. balica has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Additionally, the plant contains alkaloids like berberine and saponins like oleanolic acid, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-diarrheal effects, improving gut health and reducing the incidence of digestive disorders. The terpenoids present in Sida elongata var. balica, such as limonene and pinene, have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, while the glycosides like aucubin and verbascoside have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, thereby helping to manage fever and pain. Overall, the medicinal constituents of Sida elongata var. balica offer a range of health benefits that can improve people's daily lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases, alleviating symptoms of various conditions, and promoting overall well-being.
